acceptance_targets: [ch06_award_buyer_triptych_repetition] Pass if a cold reader can describe the Rosa/Derek/Janine sequence as three different turns with non-overlapping functions on the page: establishment, concrete cost/risk, and future-pressure change. Fail if two of the three encounters can still be swapped without materially changing Marcus's situation or the chapter's state shift.

[ch06_craft_visit_duration_continuity] Pass if a reader can calculate one consistent duration for Marcus's visits and lies using the revised chapter alone, and that duration no longer conflicts with the June 2024 firing and post-death recruitment sequence. Fail if 'fourteen months' remains alongside the current backstory dates or any other impossible count.

[ch06_craft_lakeview_route_mismatch] Pass if the revised chapter makes Lakeview and the strip-mall handoff read as two distinct locations, with Lakeview situated off Route 27 and the buyer lot reached by a later drive south on Route 1. Fail if Route 1 still runs directly past Lakeview's entrance.

[ch06_prose_overexplains_mother_lie] Pass if, after snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:177 through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:183, the revised passage lets a reader infer why Marcus lies from the scene's physical and emotional residue without a paragraph that explicitly lists pity, self-concept, and usefulness. Fail if the narration still pauses to explain the lie after the lie has already landed.
[length_chapter_06_min_words] Chapter word count is >= 4500 while preserving heading contract.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a cold reader can describe the Rosa/Derek/Janine sequence as three different turns with non-overlapping functions on the page: establishment, concrete cost/risk, and future-pressure change. Fail if two of the three encounters can still be swapped without materially changing Marcus's situation or the chapter's state shift.
findings.problem: The chapter's middle third keeps proving the same moral argument in near-identical units: buyer arrives, Marcus recalls the medical-and-insurance dossier, pills change hands, a small hardship detail lands. Rosa establishes the chapter engine cleanly, but Derek and Janine mostly re-run it, so the chapter buys additional emphasis more than additional dramatic payload. That keeps this from feeling shortlist-level even though the material is humane and specific.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework one or more of the buyer blocks in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:61, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:85, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:125 so each retained exchange has a distinct dramatic job visible on the page. One encounter can establish the compassion economy, but a later one should force a concrete operational cost or risk for Marcus, and another should change future pressure (schedule, supply, exposure, or relationship) rather than just supplying another case history. If a scene cannot carry a different job, compress its dossier material into the money-count aftermath at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:151 instead of giving it a full handoff beat.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a reader can calculate one consistent duration for Marcus's visits and lies using the revised chapter alone, and that duration no longer conflicts with the June 2024 firing and post-death recruitment sequence. Fail if 'fourteen months' remains alongside the current backstory dates or any other impossible count.
findings.problem: The chapter's own chronology does not support the repeated 'fourteen months' count. The text dates Marcus's firing to June 2024, his mother's death to four months later, and Harry's recruitment message to three months after that; with chapter 6 placed in early September 2026 by the continuity pack, Marcus has been operating as Lola's nephew for roughly twenty months, not fourteen. This is a visible continuity contradiction, not an inferential edge case.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the duration markers at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:3 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:177, or revise the backstory timing at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:161 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:163, so the chapter yields one coherent timeline from the June 2024 firing to this early-September-2026 visit.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised chapter makes Lakeview and the strip-mall handoff read as two distinct locations, with Lakeview situated off Route 27 and the buyer lot reached by a later drive south on Route 1. Fail if Route 1 still runs directly past Lakeview's entrance.
findings.problem: The chapter places Route 1 traffic directly past Lakeview's entrance, but the continuity sheet fixes Lakeview off Route 27 and separately places Marcus's buyer lot twenty minutes south on Route 1. As written, the geography collapses two established locations into one and makes the travel logic wobble.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the location phrasing in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:51, and adjust snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:57 only if needed, so Lakeview's entrance geography matches the continuity-sheet placement off Route 27 while preserving the later drive south to the Route 1 strip-mall lot.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, after snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:177 through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:183, the revised passage lets a reader infer why Marcus lies from the scene's physical and emotional residue without a paragraph that explicitly lists pity, self-concept, and usefulness. Fail if the narration still pauses to explain the lie after the lie has already landed.
findings.problem: The scene already lands when Marcus's throat closes and he lies to Lola about his mother, but the next paragraph translates the moment into abstract explanation: pity, identity, usefulness, the reason he keeps the lie moving. That post-arrival paraphrase drains force from a beat that was stronger when it stayed embodied.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite or compress the explanatory paragraph at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:185, and adjust the handoff into snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:187 if needed, so Marcus's reason for maintaining the lie is carried by behavior, image, sensory residue, or one sharply concrete thought rather than a full abstract summary.

findings.acceptance_test: Chapter word count is >= 4500 while preserving heading contract.
findings.rewrite_direction: Add causally necessary scene material that strengthens the chapter engine, increases pressure, and lands the planned state shift without filler.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This is a dark comedy about elderly agency, institutional failure, and moral compromise in a setting most fiction treats as a punchline or a tragedy but never both. The risks the novel must take: render the comedy of octogenarians as pharmaceutical distributors without condescending to them or turning them into lovable rogues; make Carmen's escalating complicity fully legible without moralizing about it; show the bodily realities of aging — incontinence, tremor, cognitive decline, falls, the smell of a body that doesn't leave a building — with unsentimental specificity; render the economic desperation of both healthcare workers and uninsured buyers without turning either into social-issue abstractions; give the moral ambiguity of the ring genuine weight by refusing to resolve it into either 'they were wrong' or 'they were heroes.' The novel must trust readers to sit with complexity. When in doubt between too raw and too safe, choose raw. A nursing home that smells like potpourri is a nursing home written by someone who's never been in one.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism for bodily functions, aging, economic shame, institutional dysfunction, or the realities of nursing home care is a craft failure. The novel's comedy and humanity depend on rendering what polite fiction glosses over: what it smells like to clean someone who can't clean themselves, what it costs to count coins for a bus fare, what oxycodone sells for per milligram on the secondary market, what a concussion looks like on an 88-year-old skull, what it sounds like when someone with Parkinson's tries to hold a coffee cup steady, what Burt's dementia actually looks like up close. If the prose flinches from these specifics, the novel loses its authority.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the default for all dialogue. Uncontracted forms appear only for emphasis, character-specific formality (Agnes's low contraction level), Carmen under extreme stress reverting to formal speech, or deliberate register choices. In a contemporary New Jersey nursing home, stiff diction is the anomaly, not the norm.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate high-intensity scenes with reflective interiority. Vary scene count: single-scene chapters for maximum compression, three-scene chapters for range. Comic chapters (3, 9) should feel structurally different from quiet character studies (7, 8, 12, 15), which should feel different from crisis chapters (11, 16, 17). Follow a confrontation with a reflection, an escalation with a domestic scene, a comic set piece with an elegiac one. If two consecutive chapters share the same rhythm, one needs redesigning.
prose_style_profile.diction: Grounded and specific. Drug names (oxycodone, alprazolam, hydroxychloroquine), nursing procedures (med pass, pill count, charting, fall assessment), body realities (incontinence, tremor, laceration, scalp bleed). No purple prose, no literary performance. The style should feel like someone who works with their hands is telling you the truth about what she saw.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, varying by POV character. Carmen chapters are warm and sensory, anchored in the body and the immediate environment. Harry chapters are precise and calculating, with attention to numbers and angles. Agnes chapters are clinical and compressed. Lola chapters are winding and emotionally layered, with embedded memories. Earl chapters are spare and weighted, with silence doing as much work as language. Marcus chapters are kinetic, with physical movement and code-switching. Tammy chapters are flat and observational, her trained eye cataloguing what doesn't add up.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied by chapter and POV. Carmen chapters favor medium sentences with occasional long runs when she's anxious. Harry chapters are short and clipped. Comic set pieces (chapters 3 and 9) move between fast inventory and slow domestic beats. Quiet chapters (7, 12, 15) slow down and let space onto the page. No chapter should feel rhythmically identical to the one before it.
